top_stories.title: SailPoint to Acquire Entro in Reported $200 Million Deal
top_stories.summary: SailPoint is acquiring Israel-based Entro Security in a reported $200 million deal. Entro specializes in non-human identity (NHI) and secrets security, and the acquisition will expand SailPoint's identity governance capabilities into machine and service account credential management.

top_stories.title: What is new in Microsoft Entra: June 2026 updates
top_stories.summary: Microsoft Entra's June 2026 update introduces phishing-resistant MFA for Linux desktops, expanded passkey support on Windows, and changes to Self-Service Password Reset identity verification. The update also includes new Conditional Access enforcement changes.

top_stories.title: IBM and Microsoft partner to automate identity threat response
top_stories.summary: IBM and Microsoft have launched a collaborative service that integrates Microsoft's security telemetry with IBM's managed response services to automate identity threat detection and remediation. The partnership is designed to bridge the gap between identifying identity-based threats and executing governed remediation actions.

top_stories.title: Microsoft enhances Entra and Defender to counter AI-driven identity attacks
top_stories.summary: Microsoft has enhanced its Entra and Defender products to counter AI-driven identity attacks. The updates address AI-automated reconnaissance, personalized social engineering, and vulnerability chaining targeting identity systems.

top_stories.title: Novo Nordisk Breach Exposes Software Development Pipeline Risk
top_stories.summary: A leaked GitHub token at Novo Nordisk exposed vulnerabilities in the company's software development pipeline. The incident highlights secrets management as an identity problem, where unmanaged machine identities and tokens represent a core IAM risk rather than merely a tooling issue.

news.title: Klue OAuth breach victim list grows as Icarus hackers claim attack
news.summary: Klue, a market intelligence platform, confirmed a security breach in which threat actors stole OAuth tokens used to access customers' Salesforce environments. The attack was claimed by a new extortion group called "Icarus," with the victim list continuing to grow.

news.title: Every AI Agent Is an Identity. Most Organizations Don't Treat Them That Way
news.summary: Token Security has published an analysis arguing that AI agents represent a new class of identity requiring governance, as they can access data, trigger workflows, deploy code, and interact with critical business systems. The piece frames AI agents as non-human identities that most organizations are not currently managing within their IAM or identity governance frameworks.

news.title: Webinar: How attackers bypass MFA and how defenders can respond
news.summary: A webinar addresses how modern phishing techniques, including Device Code phishing, can bypass MFA protections to gain unauthorized access to corporate accounts. The session covers behavioral AI as a detection and response mechanism for identifying compromised accounts when MFA has been circumvented.

news.title: Klue breach lead to Salesforce data theft, Huntress affected
news.summary: A breach at Klue, a market intelligence platform, resulted in Salesforce data theft affecting multiple companies including cybersecurity vendor Huntress. The attack began with a single compromised integration credential and cascaded across connected platforms in what Huntress described as a "security domino effect."

news.title: Forget traffic lights, Google’s reCAPTCHA may ask for hand gestures
news.summary: Google has introduced hand gesture verification as a new challenge method for its reCAPTCHA service, which is used to distinguish human users from bots. The feature is part of Google Cloud Fraud Defense and is commonly deployed on login pages, registration forms, and password reset pages.

news.title: Forget Data Leakage: Shadow AI's Real Threat Is Access Control
news.summary: An analysis piece argues that shadow AI has evolved from a data leakage problem into an access control challenge for enterprises. The article focuses on how unauthorized AI tools create identity and access management risks rather than simply data exposure risks.

news.title: Salesforce Disables Klue App Integration After OAuth Token Abuse Exposes Customer Data
news.summary: Salesforce disabled the Klue Battlecards app integration after a security incident on June 11, 2026, involving OAuth token abuse that exposed customer data. The incident resulted in Salesforce blocking the app's access to its platform via OAuth until further notice, preventing organizations from connecting Salesforce to Klue.

news.title: FIFA Bug Exposes World Cup Streams to Remote Takeover
news.summary: A security vulnerability in FIFA's systems exposed World Cup live streams to potential remote takeover due to unenforced Microsoft Entra access controls. The flaw allowed unauthorized access that could have enabled a hacker to manipulate official broadcasts, highlighting a misconfiguration in Entra-based authorization policies.

news.title: Klue OAuth breach linked to 'Icarus' Salesforce data theft attacks
news.summary: Klue, a market intelligence platform, suffered an OAuth breach that allowed threat actors known as "Icarus" to steal Salesforce CRM data from multiple organizations. The attack is part of an ongoing extortion campaign exploiting compromised OAuth credentials to gain unauthorized access to connected third-party systems.

news.title: Orphaned AI Agents: How to Find Hidden Access Risks Inside Your Network
news.summary: The article addresses the emerging IAM challenge of orphaned AI agents that retain standing privileges after their creators leave an organization. It focuses on authorization accountability gaps and access governance risks introduced by autonomous AI tools operating within enterprise environments.

news.title: Zero-Touch OAuth for MCP
news.summary: The Model Context Protocol (MCP) has released a stable Enterprise-Managed Authorization extension that allows organizations to centrally provision MCP server access through their identity provider. The extension eliminates per-app OAuth flows by enabling users to receive pre-connected MCP servers automatically upon first login, integrating with existing IdP infrastructure.

news.title: Google Toldl Researchers 'Nice Catch!' Then Denied Bug Bounty For Flaw It Still Hasn't Fixed
news.summary: A security researcher discovered a privilege escalation vulnerability in Google's Config Connector that Google acknowledged as high-severity but refused to pay a bug bounty for, claiming the behavior was "working as intended." The unpatched flaw involves privilege escalation, which has direct implications for access control and authorization within Google Cloud environments.

news.title: Microsoft Entra ID single sign-on for the Starburst connector in Power BI (Preview)
news.summary: Microsoft has released a preview feature enabling Entra ID single sign-on for the Starburst connector in Power BI. When enabled, report viewers querying semantic models in DirectQuery mode authenticate to Starburst using their own Entra ID identity, subject to Starburst's access policies including row-level and column-level security.

news.title: Semperis Appoints Jimmy McNary as Deputy Federal CTO to Drive Identity Security Strategy Across the Public Sector
news.summary: Semperis has appointed Jimmy McNary as Deputy Federal CTO to lead identity security strategy for its public sector division. McNary is a cybersecurity veteran with 25+ years of experience, previously serving as VP of Federal Solutions.

news.title: Sweeping Credential-Harvesting Heist Compromises 30K+ Fortinet Devices
news.summary: A large-scale credential-harvesting campaign has compromised over 30,000 Fortinet devices across nearly 200 countries. Attackers have compiled a list of working credentials from the breached devices, which span multiple sectors globally.

news.title: Microsoft Confirms RoguePlanet Defender Zero-Day, Says Patch is in Development
news.summary: Microsoft has disclosed a zero-day vulnerability in Microsoft Defender, designated CVE-2026-50656 with a CVSS score of 7.8, codenamed RoguePlanet. The flaw is a privilege escalation vulnerability in the Microsoft Malware Protection Engine, with a patch currently in development.

news.title: The Top 10 Attack Surface Exposures in 2026
news.summary: The article discusses top attack surface exposures predicted for 2026, using examples like credential theft and session token exposure from vulnerabilities. It covers general cybersecurity attack surface topics with only peripheral relevance to IAM concepts like credential management and authentication.

news.title: IDMWORKS Transforms Identity and Access Management with Innovative Accelerator
news.summary: IDMWORKS has launched Identity Matrix, a patent-pending delivery accelerator that consolidates four enterprise identity projects into a single platform. The tool includes built-in security checks, privileged-account discovery, and machine-identity classification capabilities.

news.title: Show HN: AuthPlane – OAuth 2.1 and PKCE authorization server for MCP
news.summary: AuthPlane is a new open-source OAuth 2.1 and PKCE authorization server designed for the Model Context Protocol (MCP). The project was shared on Hacker News with minimal engagement, receiving 4 points and no comments.

news.title: Why Account Takeovers Are Rising and How to Stop Them
news.summary: Specops Software published an analysis on the rise of account takeover attacks, focusing on techniques such as phishing, session hijacking, and MFA fatigue. The piece examines how device trust and continuous verification serve as countermeasures to reduce account takeover risk.

news.title: Securden Expands Identity Platform with AI Agent Security and Governance
news.summary: Securden has expanded its identity platform to include AI agent security and governance capabilities. The updated unified platform combines AI discovery, runtime security enforcement, shadow AI control, identity and privilege management, and continuous red teaming to manage human, machine, and AI identities.

news.title: Delinea Named a Growth and Innovation Leader in Frost Radar™: Privileged Access Management 2026
news.summary: Delinea has been named a Growth Index Leader and Innovation Index Leader in the Frost Radar™: Privileged Access Management 2026 report. The recognition places Delinea among top performers in the PAM market across both growth and innovation dimensions.

news.title: What’s new in Databricks Platform security and compliance at Data + AI Summit 2026
news.summary: Databricks announced new security and compliance features at its Data + AI Summit 2026, including Automatic Identity Management (AIM) for Microsoft Entra ID now generally available on AWS and GCP, and AIM for Okta entering Public Preview. The updates also include new Context-Based Ingress policies aimed at securing AI applications and serverless connectivity.

news.title: Aembit Extends IAM for Agentic AI to Microsoft Copilot Studio
news.summary: Aembit announced support for Microsoft Copilot Studio at Identiverse 2026, extending its IAM capabilities to Microsoft's enterprise AI agent platform. The integration focuses on identity and access management for agentic AI workloads within Copilot Studio.

news.title: Access control flaw left FIFA World Cup match streams wide-open
news.summary: A security researcher discovered an access control vulnerability in the FIFA World Cup streaming infrastructure that left live match streams exposed. The flaw allowed unauthorized access to camera controls and live stream keys due to improper authorization enforcement.

news.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
news.summary: Hackers exploited Meta's AI support assistant bot to bypass account security and reset passwords, briefly seizing high-profile Instagram accounts including the Obama White House and U.S. Space Force's Chief Master Sergeant. Instructions circulating on Telegram demonstrated how to social-engineer the AI bot into performing unauthorized password resets, circumventing Meta's authentication controls.

news.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
news.summary: A CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and other agency secrets to a public GitHub account, resulting in a significant credential leak. Lawmakers from both chambers of Congress are demanding answers from CISA while the agency works to invalidate the leaked credentials and contain the breach.

news.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
news.summary: A CISA contractor publicly exposed credentials to highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and numerous internal CISA systems via a public GitHub repository. The leaked files included details on how CISA builds, tests, and deploys software internally, representing a significant government credential exposure incident.
